title: erythroxylum membranaceum
intro: Erythroxylum membranaceum, also known as the white coca or Andean coca, is a plant species native to South America, closely related to the coca plant (Erythroxylum coca) but with a thinner leaf membrane. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, valued for its medicinal properties which include pain relief, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Erythroxylum membranaceum include reducing fever, alleviating headaches and migraines, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and steeped in hot water to make a tea, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
